source.sourcePath: raw/sources/RA/04_제품별 인허가 진행 문서/02_ADD (HAD1417MCW)/▶ CE MDD/■ 8. ADD(HAD1417MCW) H&abyz 인증관련 자료  20220203 (CE Dimdi)/4. TCF/07. Performance Test Report/07-01. EN 62220-1-1 Test report (Doc.No. A-TCF-5-058).docx

document.title: EN 62220-1-1 Test Report For Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector ADD Abyz Digital Detector

sourceSummary.oneLine: Test report for ADD Abyz Digital Detector performance measurements under EN 62220-1-1, including MTF, DQE, physical characteristics, exposure characteristics, and detector safety references.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Supports technical construction file or regulatory submissions by documenting digital X-ray detector performance and characteristics.
keyPoints: Test report was provided for Solid-State X-ray Imaging Devices, including MTF and DQE measurements tested by EN 62220-1-1(2015).
The device is described as a static X-ray detector fixed at 1 fps.
The active area is 345.24 mm x 425.6 mm and no under-scanning is possible.
Measured DQE values for ADD are listed at 0.1, 0.5, 1, 2, and 3 lp/mm.
The ADD meets regulatory standards and IEC standards as outlined in safety report 397950 and EMC reports NK-20-E-0336 and NK-20-E-0337.

concepts.evidence: Provide an estimate of Detective Quantum Efficiency (DQE) at low spatial and temporal frequency.

concepts.name: Scintillator

concepts.evidence: Describe how x-ray beam alignment and collimation to the “active” area of the device will be achieved to meet the requirements of 21CFR1020.31 or .32.

evidence.claim: The document is an EN 62220-1-1 test report for a Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector ADD Abyz Digital Detector.
evidence.evidenceText: EN 62220-1-1 Test Report For Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector ADD Abyz Digital Detector

evidence.claim: The report number and revision are A-TCF-5-058 Rev.0.
evidence.evidenceText: Report No.: A-TCF-5-058 (Rev.0)

evidence.claim: Revision 0 was established on Aug/5th/2020.
evidence.evidenceText: Rev. No. 0 Rev. date Aug/5th/2020 Revision contents Establishment

evidence.claim: The report was approved by Quality Manager Hur Namkyu on Aug/7th/2020.
evidence.evidenceText: Approval Quality Manager Hur Namkyu Aug/7th/2020

evidence.claim: The report includes MTF and DQE measurements tested by EN 62220-1-1(2015).
evidence.evidenceText: Test report was provided for Solid-State X-ray Imaging Devices, which includes MTF and DQE measurements as tested by EN 62220-1-1(2015)

evidence.claim: The applicable listed standard is EN 62220-1-1 Edition 1.0 2015-03 for determining DQE of detectors used in radiographic imaging.
evidence.evidenceText: 12-289 EN 62220-1-1 Edition 1.0 2015-03Medical electrical equipment-Characteristics of digital X-ray imaging devices Part 1-1: Determination of the detective quantum efficiency Detectors used in radiographic imaging.

evidence.claim: The detector entire dimensions including outer short ring are 384 mm x 460 mm.
evidence.evidenceText: Entire dimensions include outer short ring 384 (X) mm x 460 (Y) mm

evidence.claim: The detector panel size with outer short ring removed is 358.4 mm x 430 mm.
evidence.evidenceText: Panel size (outer short ring removed) 358.4 mm x 430 mm

evidence.claim: The active pixel area size is 345.24 mm x 425.6 mm.
evidence.evidenceText: Active pixel area size 345.24 mm x 425.6 mm

evidence.claim: The number of active area pixels is 2466 x 3040, totaling 7,496,640.
evidence.evidenceText: Number of active area pixel 2466 x 3040 = 7,496,640

evidence.claim: The detector uses CsI scintillator and passive pixel TFT panel architecture.
evidence.evidenceText: Scintillator CsI Converting X-ray to Visible light to charge Phot-Diode TFT Panel Basically, Pixel Arrays Pixel Architecture : Passive pixel

evidence.claim: The device frame rate is 1 fps.
evidence.evidenceText: Frame Rate 1 fps

evidence.claim: The device is fixed at 1 fps because it is a static X-ray detector.
evidence.evidenceText: This device is fixed at 1 fps because it is a static x-ray detector.

evidence.claim: All image data are transmitted and stored as 14-bit little-endian two-dimensional arrays without a specific header.
evidence.evidenceText: All image data shall be transmitted and stored as 14-bits little-endian (Intel bytes order) two- dimensional arrays without any specific header.

evidence.claim: The X-ray absorber scintillator converts X-ray energy directly to light.
evidence.evidenceText: X-ray absorber(scintillator) is commercially available and converts x-ray energy directly to light.

evidence.claim: The scintillator wavelength is 540 nm in the visible region.
evidence.evidenceText: This absorbers wavelength is 540nm(Visible region).

evidence.claim: ADD DQE criteria are at least 45% at 0.1 lp/mm, 40% at 0.5 lp/mm, 30% at 1.0 lp/mm, 25% at 2.0 lp/mm, and 20% at 3.0 lp/mm.
Measured ADD DQE values are 49.5% at 0.1 lp/mm, 43.8% at 0.5 lp/mm, 39.7% at 1.0 lp/mm, 31.6% at 2.0 lp/mm, and 21.5% at 3.0 lp/mm.
The DQE measurement irradiation condition was RQA5 at 70 kV, 40 mAs, SID 150 cm, and Al 21 mm filter.
evidence.evidenceText: *Irradiation condition : RQA5(70kV, 40 mAs) SID : 150cm, Al 21mm filter

evidence.claim: The detector pitch is 0.14 mm, corresponding to a sampling cut-off frequency of 3.57 lp/mm.
evidence.evidenceText: Its pitch of this system is 0.14 mm corresponding to a sampling cut-off frequency of 3.57 lp/mm.

evidence.claim: Artifacts due to aliasing are stated to be negligible.
evidence.evidenceText: Therefore, artifacts in the image due to aliasing will be negligible.

evidence.claim: No evidence of aliasing was observed in a test pattern image acquired with the detector.
evidence.evidenceText: In addition, no evidence of aliasing was observed in a test pattern image acquired with this detector

evidence.claim: The active area is in full use and no under-scanning is possible.
evidence.evidenceText: The active area of this device is 345.24 mm x 425.6 mm and is in full use and no under-scanning is possible.

evidence.claim: The X-ray system is designed to be installed with the detector input perpendicular to the X-ray beam axis.
evidence.evidenceText: The X-ray system is designed to be installed with the detector input perpendicular to the X-ray beam axis.

evidence.claim: Actual lag or residual signal measurement is 0.00012518.
evidence.evidenceText: Actual measurements : 0.00012518

evidence.claim: Readout after X-ray exposure requires at least 10 seconds until the detector is ready for the next exposure.
evidence.evidenceText: Readout of the detector after X-ray exposure requires at least 10 seconds at which time the detector is ready for the next exposure.

evidence.claim: Image readout, transferring, and pre-processing require under 5 seconds.
evidence.evidenceText: Image readout, transferring and pre-processing requires under 5 seconds.

evidence.claim: Stability time is 5 seconds.
evidence.evidenceText: - Stability time : 5 sec.

evidence.claim: The ADD meets regulatory standards as outlined in safety report 397950 and EMC reports NK-20-E-0336 and NK-20-E-0337.
evidence.evidenceText: The ADD meets the regulatory standards as outlined in the ADD safety report: 397950, EMC report: NK-20-E-0336, NK-20-E-0337

evidence.claim: The ADD meets IEC standards as outlined in safety report 397950 and EMC reports NK-20-E-0336 and NK-20-E-0337.
evidence.evidenceText: The ADD meets the IEC standards as outlined in the ADD safety report: 397950, EMC report: NK-20-E-0336, NK-20-E-0337

evidence.claim: Linearity and sensitivity for ADD are reported as 189.73 LSB/uGy with equation 189.73x + 18.451.
MTF values for HAD1417CW of ADD are 96.7% at 0.1 lp, 83.6% at 0.5 lp, 63.2% at 1 lp, 30.9% at 2 lp, and 14.7% at 3 lp.
NPS values for HAD1417CW of ADD are 10.6 at 0.1 lp, 8.4 at 0.5 lp, 6.2 at 1 lp, 3.5 at 2 lp, and 1.9 at 3 lp.
DQE values for HAD1417CW of ADD are 49.5% at 0.1 lp, 43.8% at 0.5 lp, 39.7% at 1 lp, 31.6% at 2 lp, and 21.5% at 3 lp.

reviewFlags.reason: Document date is inferred from approval date, while revision history lists Aug/5th/2020 and approval lists Aug/7th/2020.
Source metadata authoritySignal is FDA, but the source text does not explicitly name FDA; it only references 21CFR1020.31 or .32.
Source path references CE MDD and HAD1417MCW, while the source text identifies ADD, Abyz Digital Detector, and HAD1417CW.
Some equations and figures appear omitted or degraded in preprocessing, including DQE formula, noise modeling formula, and line chart/test pattern images.
Regulatory and IEC compliance statements cite supporting safety and EMC report numbers, but the detailed contents of those reports are not included in this source text.
